keyframes.pose: CONVENTION FOR ALL FRAMES: face-on view, right-handed golfer, target = screen LEFT (mirror freely). Two pivots drive everything: ARM group rotates around a shoulder pivot; CLUB rotates around a hands pivot (wrist hinge = where lag lives). 0° = shaft pointing straight down at the ball; positive = club head rising on the trail (screen-right) side. POSE: feet shoulder-width, knees flexed ~15°, spine tilted forward ~30° at the hips, arms hanging, head DOWN looking at the ball (ball slightly lead-of-center). Add one micro-waggle cycle (club head ±3°) or a breathing bob as living-idle anticipation.
keyframes.club: Arm group 0°, wrist hinge 0° — shaft points down to the ball, near vertical

keyframes.pose: One-piece move: arms + shoulders rotate together, club head sweeps LOW along the ground to the trail side ('low and slow'). Hips barely move yet. Ease-in timing — this is the slowest-feeling segment.
keyframes.club: Arm group → +45°; wrist hinge → +40° (shaft horizontal, club head waist-high on trail side)
keyframes.weight: 45/55 — just starting to load trail leg

keyframes.pose: Torso coils: shoulders turn ~90° while hips turn only ~45° (in 2D: chest compresses away from target, lead shoulder dips under the chin — this hip/shoulder separation is the visible 'coil'). Lead arm straight across the chest, lead knee kicks slightly inward, hips shift a few px trail-ward, head stays CENTERED and DOWN. Decelerating ease-out into the top — the club should visibly slow as it arrives.
keyframes.club: Arm group → +100–110° (hands above trail shoulder); wrist hinge → +90° (shaft just past vertical, club head pointing toward target above the head)

keyframes.pose: THE tempo beat (~150ms): club visually HOLDS at the top — may even drift +3–5° more (settle/overlap) — while the hips bump 5–10px toward the target and the lead knee drives target-ward FIRST. Shoulders still closed. Lower-body-starts-while-club-finishes is the single frame pair that sells a real swing.
keyframes.club: Arm group holds at top; wrist hinge deepens to ~95–100° (squash/anticipation)
keyframes.weight: 40/60 — weight already flowing back to lead side while the club is still up

keyframes.pose: FAST (~200ms — backswing-to-downswing duration MUST be ~3:1 per Tour Tempo / Yale research; tour pros: ~0.75s back, ~0.25s down). Hips fire first and open toward target, torso unwinds, arms drop, trail elbow tucks to the ribs, head stays down/behind the ball. STAGING: use only 1–2 in-betweens plus a club SMEAR — a stretched arc-shaped blur/wedge (60–90° of trailing arc) along the swing path — rather than many frames. Optionally flex the shaft 5–10° backward (drag) for squash-and-stretch.
keyframes.club: Arm group falls +110° → +10°, but wrist hinge HOLDS ~90° until the hands pass waist height (LAG), then releases rapidly in the final frames

keyframes.pose: FREEZE 1–2 frames (~40–60ms hold). Hips open ~45° to target, shoulders near square (still behind the hips), lead leg posted straight, head DOWN and behind the ball, hands slightly AHEAD of the ball. Accents: 4–6-ray star-burst at the ball, 1–2 grass/divot tufts, 1–2px body or camera shake. The ball departs on this frame.
keyframes.club: Arm group 0° (shaft at ball) with ~10° forward lean toward target (hands lead the club head); wrist hinge fully released to 0°
keyframes.weight: 80/20 — posted on lead leg, trail heel beginning to lift

keyframes.pose: Both arms extend long toward the target ('chase the ball'), torso keeps rotating, trail heel peels off the ground, and ONLY NOW does the head release up to track the ball flight. Ease-out begins after the violent downswing.
keyframes.club: Arm group → −90° (lead side, shaft horizontal then rising); wrist re-hinges to ~−45°

keyframes.pose: Belt buckle + chest face the TARGET, body fully upright in a slight reverse-C, club wrapped behind the neck over the lead shoulder, trail foot balanced on its toe (sole visible to camera), head up watching the ball. Overshoot the pose by 3–5° then settle back (recoil), then HOLD perfectly balanced to the end — a held, balanced finish is the universal 'good swing' read. Ball continues flying through this whole segment.
keyframes.club: Arm group −110 to −120°; wrist hinge −90° (club head hangs behind the shoulders)
keyframes.weight: 95/5 — nearly all on lead foot; trail toe is a kickstand only

result.ballFlight: Ball departs exactly on the impact frame (~65% of timeline). LAUNCH ANGLE: real drivers launch 10–17° above horizontal with a ~90–100ft tour apex; for a small cartoon canvas exaggerate to 25–35° for readability (use 15–20° if the stage is wide). ARC: a parabola whose apex sits slightly PAST the midpoint of horizontal travel, with a descent STEEPER than the launch — land at roughly 1.5–2x the launch angle (e.g. launch 20°, land 35–40°). A perfectly symmetric parabola reads as a lob wedge, not a drive. SPEED: fastest at launch, easing out along the path — drive path-progress with something like cubic-bezier(0.15, 0.6, 0.45, 1). TRAIL: a tapered motion streak or 2–3 fading ghost dots behind the ball, longest immediately after launch and shrinking toward the apex; optionally leave a thin tracer arc line that fades 300–500ms after the ball passes. EXIT: let the ball exit the canvas before the finish-hold ends, or land with one small bounce + short roll if the canvas allows; on a loop, fade ball + trail over the final 5%. Tiny detail that sells it: ball is invisible pre-impact only if the camera is tight — otherwise keep it sitting at the 6-o'clock ball position from frame 0 so impact has a subject.
authenticityCues: CRITICAL 1 — 3:1 TEMPO WITH A BEAT AT THE TOP. The backswing must take ~3x as long as the downswing (Tour Tempo frame counts 21/7–27/9 at 30fps; independently confirmed by Yale biomechanics research; tour total ≈ 1.2s to impact). In a 1.8s cartoon: ~650–720ms backswing (ease-out into the top), ~150ms transition pause, ~200ms downswing. Equal back/down speed is the #1 instant-fake tell — amateurs are 4:1–5:1, robots are 1:1.

CRITICAL 2 — GROUND-UP SEQUENCE + VISIBLE WEIGHT SHIFT. Hips bump and open toward the target BEFORE the club leaves the top (lower body leads by ~0.08–0.15s; in cartoon terms: hips move 1–2 frames before the arms drop). Weight visibly loads the trail leg at the top (25/75) and posts onto the lead leg through impact (80/20+), ending belt-buckle-to-target with the trail heel up and the pose HELD in balance. An arms-only swing on a static body reads as a toy windmill no matter how good the arc is.

CRITICAL 3 — WRIST LAG + HEAD DOWN THROUGH IMPACT. Keep the club-to-forearm hinge at ~90° deep into the downswing (until hands pass waist height), then release fast so the hands LEAD the clubhead at impact (~10° forward shaft lean). The head stays down/behind the ball at impact and only lifts in the follow-through. The two most common animation mistakes: 'casting' (arm + club rotating as one rigid stick, killing the whip) and lifting the head on the downswing.
Secondary — coil read: shoulders turn ~2x the hips going back (90° vs 45°); show it as chest compression + lead shoulder tucking under the chin.

Secondary — downswing staging: 1–2 in-betweens max plus an arc-shaped smear/motion wedge on the club; optionally bend the shaft 5–10° backward (drag/squash-stretch). More frames make it slower and LESS believable.
Secondary — impact punctuation: 1–2-frame freeze, star-burst at the ball, divot/grass tuft, 1–2px shake. The hold is what makes a 200ms downswing legible.
Secondary — clubhead traces one consistent circle around a stable head/sternum; a wobbling swing center reads as drunk, a fixed one reads as trained.
